title: Plug Power's Revenue Growth is Accelerating. Is it Time to Buy the Hydrogen Stock?
text: Plug Power reported Q1 revenue of $163.5 million, representing a 22% increase year-over-year—an acceleration from the prior quarter's 17.6% growth rate. This surpassed analyst expectations of around $140 million. The company achieved significant margin improvements through its Project Quantum Leap initiative, delivering a 71% margin expansion. While operating losses still totaled roughly $109 million, this represented substantial progress from the $178 million loss in the year-ago period. Revenue expansion came from three primary sources: strong demand from major customers like Walmart and Amazon in materials handling, growth in electrolyzer solutions with over 320 MW deployed globally, and increased hydrogen fuel sales. The electrolyzer business presents meaningful opportunity, with $8 billion of additional projects in the pipeline across industrial and energy applications. Management targets positive EBITDA by the fourth quarter of this year, followed by positive operating income by year-end 2027 and full profitability by 2028. The article characterizes Plug Power as high-risk, high-reward. While the stock surged over 300% in the past year and fundamentals are improving, the company remains unprofitable and highly volatile. The article suggests only investors with a high risk tolerance should consider this hydrogen stock.
